checks.finding: All 10 tiers consistent with actual scope per risk-classification-policy.md sentinel surfaces. High tasks each name a sensitive surface: T-001/T-002 command-execution/injection chokepoint (probe-engine allowlist, input schema; security-spec B1/B2), T-003 secrets/quasi-PII leakage (REQ-005, Information Disclosure), T-006 installer MCP-placement data-mutation (REQ-007) + Node<20 gate, T-007/T-008 user IDE-config file mutation (~/.cursor/mcp.json, VS Code; B3, ADR-0005), T-009 destructive uninstall of user entries (REQ-010, B3). T-004 medium (dist/CI, no sensitive surface, CI-detectable). T-005/T-010 low are genuinely doc/traceability-only with no control-flow/data/security impact - no over-classification. No auth/payment/PII/migration/external-contract surface is under-classified.

No critical-tier task exists (highest is high), so second-approver/signed-bundle not required. All 7 high tasks (T-001,T-002,T-003,T-006,T-007,T-008,T-009) carry both mandatory Done When items: 'Red->Green evidence 記録(tdd)' and '独立レビュー verdict PASS が evidence に記録される' (e.g., T-001 lines 40-41, T-007 lines 237-238, T-009 lines 299-300). All also declare Required Workflow: tdd, consistent with risk-gate-matrix high tier.

No oversized task: Done When item counts are T-001=7, T-002=7, T-003=5, T-004=6, T-005=5, T-006=6, T-007=7, T-008=5, T-009=5, T-010=4 - none exceed 8. Titles use '+' to join cohesive sub-parts of one area (e.g., T-001 foundation/probe-engine/envelope are one server-foundation area), not sequential multi-phase work. No fragmentation: there are not >=3 tasks each a single trivial edit; every task is a substantive unit.

Every functional task carries an edge/error-path AC: T-001->AC-004 (timeout 2s/8KiB output/kill error-path), T-002->AC-003 (allowlist外 -> invalid-input), T-003->AC-005 (canary secret non-leak negative), T-006->AC-009 + Node<20 gate edge (Done When line 203), T-007->AC-015 (broken-JSON failsafe), T-008->AC-013 (parity incl. Node<20 + failsafe), T-009->AC-012 (other user entries intact). requirements.md Edge Cases (uninstalled CLI, hang/large output, java stderr, allowlist外 name, missing/broken IDE config, Node<20) are all mapped to error-path ACs.

Declared test types match scope in acceptance-tests.md and each references a specific AC-NNN: TEST-001 unit(snapshot)->AC-001, TEST-002 integration->AC-002, TEST-003 unit+static->AC-003, TEST-004 integration(error-path)->AC-004, TEST-005 integration(no-secret grep)->AC-005, TEST-006 static(grep)->AC-006, TEST-007 smoke->AC-007, TEST-008 CI dist-parity->AC-008, TEST-009-013/015 integration(installer harness)->respective ACs, TEST-014 doc review->AC-014. Static/snapshot/smoke/integration assignments align with each target surface; tasks cite the ACs in their Done When.

All 7 high tasks carry an explicit Rollback: field: T-001 (line 18, revert + infra-spec Rollback ref), T-002 (57), T-003 (92), T-006 (189, existing path reverts), T-007 (221, uninstall/manual key removal + broken-JSON failsafe), T-008 (254), T-009 (284, 'installer 管理名のみ削除' design/test guard). infra-spec.md#rollback provides the feature-level single-revert procedure.

Shared-file writes all carry enforced Blockers ordering with distinct purposes: src/index.ts T-002(create)->T-003(add diagnostics) via T-003 Blockers T-002; install.sh + tests/install.tests.sh T-006(VALID_MCPS/MCP_LIST core + AC-009/Node-gate)->T-007(Cursor/VS Code register + AC-010/011/015) via T-007 Blockers T-005,T-006; traceability.md written only by T-010, design.md addendum by T-005. No two tasks modify the same file for the same purpose without ordering (legitimate creator->extender sharing).

Blockers reflect genuine artifact dependencies with no spurious or missing links: T-002<-T-001 (envelope/allowlist/probe-engine), T-003<-T-002 (server/index), T-004<-T-003 (bundles full src incl. diagnostics), T-006<-T-004 (installer places built dist), T-007<-T-005,T-006 (needs OQ-001-resolved registration format + installer core), T-008<-T-007 (ps1 parity of sh behavior), T-009<-T-007,T-008 (uninstall symmetric to both register paths), T-010<-all. T-005 and T-001 correctly carry no blockers (independent). Consistent with reviewer A's passing DEPENDENCY-COMPLETE and DEPENDENCY-CYCLE checks (integrated-summary).

SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. All of T-001-T-010 are greenfield feature implementation (new MCP server, installer extension, docs); none is scoped as bugfix/regression/debugging/incident remediation.
